hypothesis: Using frozen, pre-trained single-gas neural networks to generate node features for a two-node GraphSAGE model that also includes gas fraction inputs will produce more accurate and more generalizable predictions of radial etching rates for Ar/O2 mixtures (higher R² and lower weighted MSE), particularly under withheld high power/pressure and low-Ar-fraction test conditions, than a conventional feedforward baseline or other GNN architectures.
